title: US-Israel-Iran war hits oil supplies: How India is preparing for the economic fallout
content: The geopolitical strain has pushed up global oil and gas prices. For India, which relies heavily on imports, this surge translates into a higher import bill and adds to inflationary pressures. India depends on overseas purchases for almost 90 per cent of its crude oil needs.

title: US hits Iran’s ‘command & control’ sites; Trump says war ‘can be fought forever’
content: US President Donald Trump said America has an “unlimited supply” of weapons and claimed wars can be fought “forever” as tensions with Iran escalate following the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Iran has launched retaliatory strikes, including a drone attack on the US embassy in Riyadh, while the US says it destroyed key Iranian military facilities. Civilian casualties are mounting, Israel and Hezbollah continue exchanging fire, and oil prices are rising amid fears of regional escalation.

title: 'Back home and safe': PV Sindhu returns to India after being stuck in Dubai
content: Indian badminton star PV Sindhu has returned to Bengaluru safely. She was stranded in Dubai amid escalating Middle East conflict. Sindhu expressed gratitude to ground teams and Dubai authorities for their support. The athlete and her coach experienced a close call after an explosion nearby. She is now resting and planning her next steps.

title: OpenAI claims the Chinese government uses ChatGPT, and the company is quite unhappy about this
content: OpenAI alleges China's government is weaponizing ChatGPT for cyber operations, including polishing internal reports for influence campaigns targeting dissidents and foreign leaders.  Beijing's operatives reportedly forged documents and flooded platforms with fake reports to silence critics.  This misuse highlights significant AI dangers, with affected dissidents urging the tech industry to take responsibility.

title: ‘From Gaza to Minab’: Hundreds of graves being dug in Iran after school strike
content: Iran's foreign minister shared a photo of graves, alleging a US-Israeli strike on a girls' school in Minab killed over 150. Iranian authorities described it as a war crime, while the US Central Command stated they are reviewing reports of civilian harm. International law prohibits direct targeting of civilian infrastructure like schools.

title: 'Abdication, not neutrality': Sonia Gandhi slams PM Modi's silence on Khamenei assassination
content: Sonia Gandhi criticized the Modi government's silence on Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's assassination, calling it an 'abdication' and 'tacit endorsement.' She argued India's lack of a clear response, especially after PM Modi's recent Israel visit, raises doubts about its foreign policy's credibility and direction.

title: Tech employees across America send open letter to Pentagon on Anthropic
content: Hundreds of tech employees have signed an open letter urging the Department of War and Congress to withdraw Anthropic's "supply chain risk" designation. This action followed a failed contract negotiation, leading to a presidential directive to cease using Anthropic's technology. The letter argues this precedent punishes American innovation and urges a resolution through commercial channels.

title: 'Laser-focused' Op Epic Fury: US shares footage of hitting Iran 'unapologetically' — Watch
content: US Central Command has released night-vision footage showing a drone strike on a white truck in Iran under Operation Epic Fury. The video comes as US officials defend the expanding military campaign. War Secretary Pete Hegseth said the mission targets Iranian missiles and is not a regime-change war, even as regional tensions and American casualties rise-

title: Bengaluru techie dies by suicide over astrologer's prediction of separation after marriage
content: A 27-year-old Bengaluru techie allegedly died by suicide after an astrologer predicted marital discord. Distressed by the prediction, she reportedly hanged herself in her room. Her parents had consulted the astrologer regarding her inter-caste marriage, and a ritual was performed to avert the predicted issues.

title: Claude down: Anthropic’s AI tool faces second outage in less than 24 hours
content: Anthropic's AI assistant, Claude, is experiencing its second outage in 24 hours, impacting thousands of users in the US and hundreds in India. Reports indicate issues with chat, the app, and website access. The company's status page confirms 'Elevated Errors' across multiple services, with the incident currently unresolved.

title: At least 6 killed after van collides with double-decker bus in Hathras
content: According to Additional Superintendent of Police Ramanand Kushwaha,15 am. The accident involved a van travelling from Delhi to Dholpur in Rajasthan and a double-decker bus heading from Noida to Gorakhpur.

title: First-year PG medical student found hanging in Ahmedabad college hostel
content: A 26-year-old first-year orthopaedics student, Chintan Parmar, was found hanging in his hostel room at Dr M K Shah Medical College in Ahmedabad. His roommate discovered the body, and doctors attempted resuscitation but declared him dead. Preliminary inquiries suggest he was under stress, though the exact reason for the extreme step is yet to be established.

title: Strong earthquake strikes Indonesia's Sumatra island
content: A powerful 6.1-magnitude earthquake jolted off Indonesia's Sumatra island on Tuesday, a region situated on a major tectonic collision zone. This seismic activity is characteristic of the Pacific "Ring of Fire." While a previous 6.3-magnitude quake in November 2025 near Aceh caused no tsunami, the region remains prone to such events due to plate convergence.

title: US troops opened fire on protesters who stormed Karachi consulate: Report
content: US Marines reportedly opened fire on demonstrators who stormed Pakistan's Karachi consulate following the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader in American-Israeli strikes. At least 10 died in the incident, sparking nationwide protests and a ban on gatherings. The consulate claimed shots originated from within the compound amid escalating regional tensions.

title: ‘Chhoti soch hai’: Afridi tears into 50 lakh fine, demands 2-year exile for some
content: Former Pakistan captain Shahid Afridi strongly opposes fines for players after their T20 World Cup exit. He calls the proposed PKR 5 million penalty small thinking. Afridi suggests sending underperforming players to first-class cricket. Some players, he believes, should be out of the Pakistan team for two years. He also calls for better workload management and player changes.

title: PM Modi's YouTube account crosses 30 million subscribers
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has surpassed 30 million YouTube subscribers, solidifying his status as the world's most followed leader on the platform. This digital milestone follows his Instagram achievement of over 100 million followers. His significant online presence extends to X, where he boasts a substantial following, underscoring his extensive global digital outreach.

title: New satellite images capture aftermath of Iran's strike on Saudi Aramco oil refinery
content: Iran launched a barrage of drones and missiles at Gulf states, targeting energy installations in Qatar and Saudi Arabia, including the Ras Tanura Refinery. The US Embassy in Riyadh was also attacked. These strikes, continuing into Tuesday, mark a sharp escalation in regional conflict following the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

title: Blank cheques, threats & humiliation: Triple suicide horror in Hyderabad; 4 arrested
content: Four money lenders have been arrested in Hyderabad for allegedly abetting the suicide of a family of three. The victims, a couple and their son, reportedly took their lives due to relentless harassment and threats from financiers demanding repayment of a ₹57 lakh loan. Police recovered suicide notes and seized blank cheques and land documents from the deceased.

title: 'India T20 World Cup final nahi khelega': Pakistan star sparks fresh controversy
content: Mohammad Amir's prediction that India would miss the T20 World Cup semifinals backfired as Sanju Samson's unbeaten 97 guided them to a five-wicket win over the West Indies. Despite this, Amir maintains India won't reach the final, citing bowling form and batting collapses, though Samson's knock set a new record for India in a successful T20 World Cup run-chase.

title: Over 125 happy Holi wishes, messages, quotes, greetings for WhatsApp and Instagram Status
content: India's vibrant Holi festival, celebrated in spring, embodies love and camaraderie.  Marked by smearing colors and splashing water, it's a time for families and friends to unite, sharing wishes for happiness and prosperity.  The spirit of "Bura Na Mano Holi Hai" encourages lighthearted fun, making it a joyous occasion for all.

title: ‘India can’t afford to take England lightly’: McGrath warns ahead of big semi-final
content: Former Australian pacer Glenn McGrath predicts an India-South Africa T20 World Cup final. He warns India to be wary of England in the semi-finals, despite their inconsistent campaign. McGrath praised South Africa's T20 focus and highlighted Jasprit Bumrah as a crucial 'big-game player' for India's knockout chances. He also expressed little surprise at Australia's early exit.

title: Former Union minister KP Unnikrishnan passes away in Kozhikode
content: Veteran Congress leader and former Union Minister KP Unnikrishnan has passed away at the age of 90 in Kozhikode. Unnikrishnan, a long-serving parliamentarian, represented the Vadakara constituency for nearly three decades and was an influential figure in Kerala politics. Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan expressed his deep condolences.

title: Gold hits Rs 1.66 lakh, silver gains Rs 20,000 — should you buy on dips?
content: Gold and silver prices surged on Monday due to escalating Middle East geopolitical tensions, with gold reaching Rs 1.66 lakh per 10 grams and silver Rs 2.86 lakh per kg. Experts predict further record highs if the conflict persists, driven by gold's safe-haven status. Investors are capitalizing on price dips to accumulate despite the rally.

title: 'Information-hungry person': What Bill, Hillary Clinton revealed about Epstein, Trump in depositions
content: Bill and Hillary Clinton have distanced themselves from Jeffrey Epstein in recent depositions. Bill stated he ended his association with the convicted sex offender years before Epstein's 2008 plea, denying knowledge of sex trafficking. Hillary claimed no recollection of meeting Epstein. Both testified under oath, with Bill acknowledging limited travel on Epstein's jet for humanitarian work.

title: Trump open to 'boots on the ground' in Iran - what does it mean?
content: US President Donald Trump has not ruled out deploying troops to Iran if circumstances necessitate it, stating all military options remain open. While he believes ground forces are unlikely to be required, he emphasized their potential use if deemed essential.

title: Bad Bunny breaks Super Bowl viewership record despite backlash
content: Bad Bunny’s Super Bowl 60 halftime show set a new global record with more than 4.1 billion total views, the NFL announced on March 2. While his TV rating ranked fourth all time at 128.2 million viewers, digital engagement pushed him past every previous performer. Social media impressions, streaming spikes, and chart dominance on Apple Music underscored the global impact, marking a milestone moment for both the artist and the league.

title: Lucknow murder: ‘Killer' son who wrote Ramlila scripts planned perfect cover-up, say cops
content: A chilling twist has emerged in the murder of pathologist Manvendra Singh. Investigators believe the accused, Akshat, a local Ramlila scriptwriter known for intense roles, used his creative skills to fabricate a false narrative.

title: US embassy in Riyadh targeted by drones; Saudi Arabia issues shelter-in-place order for 3 cities
content: Two drones struck the US embassy in Riyadh early Tuesday, causing a small fire and minor damage. Saudi authorities confirmed the attack, with air defense systems intercepting four drones aimed at the diplomatic quarter. The US embassy issued a shelter-in-place advisory for American citizens in the region as a precautionary measure.

title: Bengaluru actor gags live-in partner with lover’s help, stabs him to death
content: An actress, her boyfriend, and his associate were arrested for the murder of her live-in partner in Bengaluru. The trio allegedly stabbed 29-year-old Mohan Krishna after an alcohol party, following disagreements over the actress's intention to marry her boyfriend. The accused lured the victim to a desolate spot for the fatal attack.

title: Inside Keerthy Suresh’s beautiful home: Her and Anthony Thattil’s ‘house of fun’
content: After fifteen years of togetherness, National Award-winning actor Keerthy Suresh and her husband, Anthony Thattil, have finally opened the doors to their sanctuary in Kochi—a vibrant, soulful space they aptly call the "House of Fun."

title: From Ukraine to Gulf: How Iran’s cheap drones are changing modern warfare
content: Iranian Shahed drones, previously seen in Ukraine, are now a common threat over the Persian Gulf, targeting multiple Gulf states and Israel following US and Israeli attacks. These cost-effective, AI-enabled drones, also produced by Russia as Gerans, are deployed in swarms to overwhelm air defenses, causing significant damage and straining defensive capabilities across the region.

title: 'India would've been knocked out': Ex-Pakistan star sparks controversy after India's semifinal entry
content: Mohammad Amir faced criticism after his prediction that India wouldn't reach the T20 World Cup 2026 semi-finals proved incorrect. Despite India's win, Amir highlighted a controversial dismissal of Shimron Hetmyer, believing it could have altered the match's outcome. He also questioned India's overall performance, citing fielding errors and reliance on Jasprit Bumrah.

title: Stock market holiday for Holi: Will NSE, BSE remain closed on March 3 or 4?
content: Dalal Street will observe a holiday on March 3rd for Holi, providing a break for traders and investors. The stock market operates on weekdays, excluding weekends and exchange-declared holidays, with specific timings for pre-open, regular trading, closing, and block deal sessions.

title: Why was Sanju Samson axed? Gautam Gambhir's big admission
content: Sanju Samson's unbeaten 97 guided India to the semi-finals, a performance head coach Gautam Gambhir attributed to a strategic break after a tough series against New Zealand. Gambhir highlighted Samson's composure and classical shots, expressing hope that this match-winning knock marks a turning point for the talented batter.

title: Rosanna Pansino questions MrBeast views after 1.45 billion claim
content: Rosanna Pansino questioned whether MrBeast’s reported 1.45 billion viewers included paid promotions after a claim by Beast Industries CEO Jeff Housenbold. She said she was citing public ad data, not making an accusation. Online reactions were divided. Neither MrBeast nor his company has responded, while his recent “negative money” comment also drew attention.

title: 'You’ll soon find out response': Trump reacts to drone attack on US embassy in Riyadh
content: US President Donald Trump has vowed swift retaliation following a drone strike on the American embassy in Riyadh. The Saudi defense ministry confirmed two drones hit the compound, causing minor damage and a small fire. This attack occurs amidst escalating tensions and Iranian strikes targeting Gulf states with US bases.

title: Every India-England semi-final winner has won the T20 World Cup — trend to continue?
content: India and England renew their T20 World Cup semi-final rivalry at Wankhede, marking their third consecutive clash in the last four. Both previous encounters saw the winner go on to lift the trophy, adding significant intrigue to this high-stakes encounter. The teams hold a balanced record in World Cup semi-finals, with one win each.

title: Water-logging near Attukal temple poses problems for devotees
content: As crowd of devotees swelled, many chose to set up their hearths on the few dry spots amid the water-logged areas near Attukal Bhagavathy temple

title: West Indies cricket team stranded in India due to international airspace restrictions
content: "Cricket West Indies [CWI] wishes to advise that the departure of the West Indies Senior Men's Team from India, following their exit from the ICC Men's T20 World Cup, has been delayed due to international airspace restrictions," says the board statement

title: Protests against Khamenei's killing break out in Rajouri, amid curbs across Kashmir
content: In Budhal, residents gathered in front of the Sheikh-ul-Alam Masjid to stage a demonstration and condemned the joint military action by the U.S. and Israel in Iran

title: Congress changes strategy, opts for senior T.N. leader to hold seat-sharing talks with DMK
content: Until now, AICC in-charge for Tamil Nadu, Girish Chodankar, who hails from Goa, was negotiating with the DMK

title: A multi-dimensional look at Kerala
content: This e-book features contributions from a distinguished panel of subject experts, including acclaimed writers, political analysts, and economists.

title: How Dravidian parties changed the course of Tamil Nadu’s progress
content: The abolition of hand-rickshaws, along with other major social reforms initiated in Tamil Nadu by a government inspired by the ideals of the Dravidian Movement, helped make the State a leader in social justice reforms in India.

title: Key accused held for firing at lawyer linked to Lawrence Bishnoi
content: The arrest comes a week after Deepak Khatri, a lawyer associated with Lawrence Bishnoi, and his associates were shot at near the ISBT in the Kashmere Gate area of north Delhi

title: Palaniswami asserts no discussion on Sasikala’s entry into NDA figured in his meeting with Amit Shah
content: Sasikala, who had all along been claiming to be the general secretary of the AIADMK, announced last week in Kamuthi of the Ramanathapuram district her decision to launch a party and unveiled the party flag

title: Bhadravati BEO, middleman caught by Lokayukta police taking bribe to clear salary dues of official
content: The complainant had requested the Block Education Officer to clear his salary dues and arrears pending for the period 2011 to 2023, for which the BE0 demanded ₹4 lakh

title: Rent a Tree, an agri-startup from Kochi, provides mango trees for lease across India
content: Rent a Tree manages 250 acres of Alphonso mango farms in Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, and Kerala, catering to over 160 customers with rentals starting at ₹10,300

title: Six killed as double-decker bus hit van on Yamuna Expressway in U.P.'s Hathras
content: The van carrying 13 passengers was on its way from Prem Nagar in Delhi to Dholpur in Rajasthan when it was hit from behind by a sleeper bus travelling from Noida to Gorakhpur, ASP Ram Anand Kushwaha said.

title: Attukal Pongala under way on a fervent note in Kerala capital as thousands of devotees offer pongala
content: Churches, mosques and residents opened their doors to devotees, who set up open brick hearths on either side of the roads and in public spaces to cook the sacrament, an offering of rice and jaggery

title: Hubballi-Dharwad Municipal Corporation to declare health insurance scheme for journalists, says Mayor Jyoti Patil
content: The Mayor inaugurated rangoli and cooking competitions organised to mark Women’s Day, at the Sabha Bhavan of Moorusavira mutt in Hubballi, on March 1

title: How Tamil Nadu got 234 Assembly seats: The Delimitation Commission’s calculated departure
content: Under Article 170 of the Constitution and Section 8(b) of the Delimitation Commission Act, 1962, the number of seats in a State Assembly must be an integral multiple of the number of Lok Sabha seats allotted to that State

title: MHA sets up two more empowered panels in West Bengal to process CAA citizenship applications
content: Empowered committee will be headed by an officer not below the rank of Deputy Secretary to the Government of India, to be nominated by the Registrar General and Census Commissioner of India

title: Shreshta IT signs MoU with National Forensic Sciences University Dharwad
content: The collaboration hopes to unite academic excellence and industry expertise toward a shared goal, and to strengthen cybersecurity capabilities, and prepare students for real-world challenges

title: West Asia crisis: SpiceJet to operate four special flights from UAE’s Fujairah
content: SpiceJet will restore its scheduled Fujairah-Delhi and Fujairah-Mumbai flights from March 4, the statement said

title: K.P. Unnikrishnan, former Union Minister and senior Congress leader, passes away
content: He was the MP for Vadakara in Kozhikode from 1971 to 1996, representing three political parties. He first entered the Upper House of Parliament in 1971 on a Congress ticket

title: Indore water contamination: Judicial panel extends deadline for submitting evidence to April 1
content: The single-member commission of former Madhya Pradesh High Court judge Justice Sushil Kumar Gupta is investigating the causes of drinking water contamination in the Bhagirathpura area, the loss of lives and medical impact on people, administrative lapses, action against those responsible and remedial measures.

title: Modi govt's silence on Khamenei assassination not neutral but abdication: Sonia Gandhi
content: When the targeted killing of a foreign leader draws no clear defence of sovereignty or international law from our country and impartiality is abandoned, it raises serious doubts about the direction and credibility of our foreign policy, says Sonia Gandhi said in her article.

title: Cuddalore’s Silver Beach, developed under Blue Flag certification programme, ready for inauguration
content: The Cuddalore Corporation completed the beautifcation and improvement works on the beach at a cost of ₹4 crore

title: Preparations started for the by-election in Tripura Assembly seat
content: The total number of voters in the constituency, according to the published roll, is 46,143; Officials say there could be some change if any fault is found in the count.

title: Race heats up for Bihar’s five Rajya Sabha seats with RJD entry
content: Though the Opposition party is likely to secure the support of Mahagathbandhan allies like the Congress and the Left parties, their combined tally still falls short of the minimum number required for winning a Rajya Sabha seat

title: Jharkhand CM urges PM to ensure safe return of stranded Indians from Gulf nations
content: Mr. Soren shared the helpline numbers for assistance: Toll-Free Helpline: 1800-3456-526; Landline: 0651-2480083, 0651-2481188, 0651-2480058; WhatsApp: 9470132591, 9431336472, 9431336398, 9431336427, 9431336432.

title: India pitches to join Future Combat Air System with France
content: India’s defence exports have reached a record high of ₹23,620 crore; it exports to over 100 nations, including to United States, France and Armenia

title: Ernakulam Rural Police seize over 100 kg of ganja in three weeks
content: The police seized 133 kg of suspected ganja in six major drug hauls reported recently. The total quantity of suspected ganja seized by the Rural police in 2025 stands at 603 kg

title: Internal quota: Dalit Left communities and STs gear up for protests in Karnataka
content: State government has asked recruitment departments/authorities to initiate recruitment for 56,432 posts with a cap of 50% in reservation and without internal reservation among Scheduled Castes

title: IndiGo to operate 10 relief flights to Jeddah, Air India Express restarts Oman services
content: Dubai-based Emirates and Flydubai, along with Abu Dhabi-based Etihad Airways, announced a limited resumption of flights, while advising passengers not to proceed to airports unless contacted by the airlines

title: GHMC’s first post-dissolution meeting approves 30 proposals
content: The first post-dissolution meeting saw approvals for a land deal priced at earlier auction rates, ₹500-crore allocations to two new corporations, and several pending city projects

title: Chhattisgarh private schools face off with government over RTE reimbursement, announce ‘non-cooperation’
content: School owners have alleged that the government is insensitive towards their long-standing demand to suitably compensate for their expenses on the education of poor children

title: Modi speaks to Saudi Crown Prince, Jordan, Bahrain Kings
content: He thanks them for looking after well-being of the Indian community in these countries; calls for earliest restoration of regional peace and stability; condemns attack on Saudi Arabia and Bahrain

title: Tamil got wide global recognition under Modi government, says C.P. Radhakrishnan
content: In a veiled attack on Dravidian politics, Vice President C. P. Radhakrishnan says certain people who argue that India was never under one administrative regime “forget that even Tamil Nadu was not a united society”

title: Religious leader announces Gau Pratistha Dharmayuddh march to Lucknow
content: March from Varanasi follows ultimatum to Uttar Pradesh government seeking cow as “State Mother” status and beef export ban

title: Toxic gas leaks from Palghar chemical unit; 2,600 evacuated, 3 persons complain of eye irritation
content: Leak from 2,500-litre oleum tank at Boisar MIDC unit spreads dense fumes across 5-km radius; 1,600 students and 1,000 workers evacuated as NDRF teams launch containment operations

title: Madras High Court sets aside order to extend Thalikku Thangam scheme to those who earn up to ₹12,000 a month
content: Chief Justice Manindra Mohan Shrivastava and Justice G. Arul Murugan say the single judge ought not to have expanded the scope of a writ petition before him

title: ‘Peace is not only a moral imperative, it is an economic necessity’
content: The assassination of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic of Iran, marked a dangerous and destabilising moment for the region and the global economy, says Syed Hakim Raza, president of Indo-Iran Chamber of Commerce and Industries

title: ED attaches assets worth ₹18.10 crore in ‘illegal’ betting app case
content: The ED has alleged that 1xBet was operating in India without authorisation and was promoting illegal online betting and gambling activities through multiple mirror websites

title: Supreme Court to look into feasibility of NAT for blood transfusion
content: Petitioner-NGO says right to safe blood transfusion is a fundamental part of the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution; it says NAT is a safer testing mechanism to ensure the supply of safe and infection-free blood to recipients

title: Congress seeks power only with public consent, not by spreading hate: Rahul Gandhi
content: Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha addresses valedictory session of  District Congress Committee presidents’ training camp in Vikarabad; says Congress was founded on the ideals of truth and harmony, and leaders must constantly strive to practice these values in public life

title: Post-SIR, Centre forms two more panels to fast-track CAA applications in West Bengal
content: Thousands of people belonging to the Matua community have applied or are being encouraged by the BJP to apply for citizenship under CAA

title: Amit Shah promises Seventh Pay Commission to West Bengal government employees
content: Union Home Minister says Mamata Banerjee’s nephew Abhishek will rule the State if Trinamool Congress is voted to power again

title: Passport office in Kochi receives hoax bomb threat
content: The Ernakulam Town South Police, along with the bomb squad, conducted a detailed inspection of the premises after being alerted

title: Assess environmental compensation from those responsible for pollution of Kadambrayar: NGT tells PCB
content: The environmental compensation will be fixed based on the ‘polluter pays principle’. The PCB has to work out the amount based on the waste generated by the violators

title: Festive mood grips Kerala capital ahead of Attukal Pongala March 3
content: Labelled as among the largest religious congregations of women in the country, thousands of families across Kerala and also neighbouring States are trickling into a six-km radius of the famed Attukal Devi Temple to offer a sacrament of rice and jaggery cooked on makeshift brick hearths

title: ₹12 crore non-plan grant for SSUS
content: The assistance has been earmarked for the payment of pension, commutation, death-cum-retirement gratuity, and terminal surrender benefits for retired teaching and non-teaching staff

title: Unfinished seat sharing clouds UDF’s campaign rollout in Kottayam
content: The prolonged delay in finalising the seat sharing formula between the Congress and Kerala Congress (Joseph) faction has effectively stalled the candidate selection process

title: Karunakar Reddy renews call for TTD chairman’s resignation, cites ‘new evidence’
content: Former MLA releases letter allegedly written by woman seen in viral video; B. R. Naidu terms charges a smear campaign and warns of legal action

title: Global Ayyappa Sangamam: Kerala HC seeks records on audit of expenses
content: Audit firm told to submit  the full statement of the account opened with Dhanlaxmi Bank in the name of the Devaswom Accounts Officer for the purpose of the global conclave by March 5, 2026

title: Pvt. school staff booked for harassing five minor girls in Kalaburagi
content: Police Commissioner urged to seek cancellation of the accused’s bail and to ensure stringent legal action against him

title: Three spectators gored to death by bulls during  manjuvirattu at Sivaganga in Tamil Nadu
content: A total of 128 bulls from Sivaganga, Dindigul, Theni, Pudukkottai, Madurai and Tiruchi districts were released from the entry point

title: CM directs officials to increase administrative efficiency using AI
content: Naidu calls for integration of drone, CCTV and satellite data to improve public services, orders safety review after blast in Kakinada district and stresses seamless pension delivery and focus on Swarna Gramams and Swarna Wards

title: Breaking the cycle of violence against women
content: Victims should be reassured that they are worthy irrespective of the attempts by the abuser to run them down, and they should be provided a safe, caring environment away from the perpetrator

title: SVU launches DGCA-approved drone pilot training course
content: University becomes the first general State university to receive official approval from DGCA to be considered as a Remote Drone Pilot Training Organisation

title: Supporters of Mallikarjun Mutya stage protest seeking withdrawal of case
content: Members of Koli-Kabbaliga Horata Samiti and followers of the Mutya of the Mahal Roja Mutt take to the streets in his support in Kalaburagi

title: No. 2 Arizona tops ISU for outright Big 12 title
content: No. 2 Arizona clinched the outright Big 12 regular-season title with a 73-57 win over No. 6 Iowa State on Monday night.

title: Trae tossed from bench ahead of Wizards debut
content: Trae Young, who is set to make his Wizards debut Thursday vs. Utah, was ejected for leaving the bench in Monday's loss to Houston.

title: Boozer, Duke 'show fight' in securing ACC title
content: Star freshman Cameron Boozer and top-ranked Duke overwhelmed NC State on Monday night to clinch the outright ACC regular-season title.

title: Giannis rusty but 'just happy that I'm out there'
content: Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo returned Monday night against the Celtics, his first game in more than five weeks after straining his right calf.

title: Source: Mack to play in '26, explore all FA options
content: Outside linebacker Khalil Mack, who had mulled retirement, will play in 2026 and explore all options in free agency, a source told ESPN, confirming a report by NFL Network.

title: AD: Pearl's dismissal of Miami (OH) disrespectful
content: Miami (Ohio) AD David Sayler challenged Bruce Pearl over what he called "disrespect" after the former Auburn coach said the RedHawks don't deserve a spot in the NCAA tournament.

title: DeRosa sets USA rotation with Webb set for G1
content: Giants ace Logan Webb will start Game 1 of the World Baseball Classic for Team USA when it takes on Brazil in pool play Friday, manager Mark DeRosa said Monday afternoon.

title: Phantom, Mist put on show, set for Unrivaled final
content: Phantom BC and Mist will face off in the Unrivaled championship game Wednesday night after both delivered entertaining semifinal wins in front of a sellout crowd at Barclays Center on Monday.

title: 📋 Kiper's top NFL combine standouts
content: Mel Kiper Jr. picked out 13 players who caught his attention during combine workouts, including Chris Brazzell II and Dani Dennis-Sutton.

title: Barnwell stacks the best defensive players available in free agency next week: Who will get big deals?
content: With one week until NFL free agency, let's put defensive players into six tiers based on their potential offseason contracts.

title: Men's Bubble Watch: Ohio State's big Sunday win gets it promoted
content: The Buckeyes upset Purdue for a much-needed Quadrant 1-A win -- and a bump up to "should be in" territory.

title: Women's Bracketology: Welcome to the top 16, Minnesota
content: Sunday's reveal put a new team in the top 16. The start of Champ Week will bring more answers as the bracket comes into focus.

title: TGL highlights: Los Angeles Golf Club advances to playoffs
content: With the season on the line, Los Angeles Golf Club beats New York Golf Club to secure a playoff spot.

title: The curious case of Sawyer Robertson, Mike Leach's last quarterback
content: It took the former Baylor signal-caller years to get comfortable running an offense. Will he be able to make things work in the NFL?

title: Harden to Cleveland: How the former MVP is transforming the Cavs
content: Entering January, the Cavaliers were stuck in a play-in spot. They've since racked up the best record in the NBA and added an 11-time All-Star.

title: Plum: WNBA's CBA offer is 'win,' but talks go on
content: Kelsey Plum said the CBA offer from the league to the WNBPA is a "significant win," but negotiations should continue and "a strike would be the worst thing for both sides."

title: How many future Hall of Famers are playing in the 2026 WBC?
content: From the locks to the long shots to those in between, we count Cooperstown candidates in this year's World Baseball Classic.

title: First title in 40 years? Inside the Mets' winter makeover
content: Saying goodbye to the team's four longest-tenured players was tough. But New York is turning the page.

title: UConn finishes off perfect 31-0 regular season
